query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
Research complete. Here is a summary of the findings on the 2026 Johor state election:

## Bottom Line
**BN is the clear favorite to retain government**, with analyst consensus pointing to BN winning between the mid-30s and low-40s of the 56 seats. The critical question is not *whether* BN wins, but whether it can **defend its 2022 supermajority of 40 seats** — anything below that would be read as underperformance.

## Key Dynamics

**Timeline & structure:** Polling is 11 July 2026 (early voting 7 July); assembly dissolved 1 June, ~a year early. 29 seats needed for a majority. ~2.72m voters, just over half Malay.

**Expected outcome:** One concrete forecast (Oriental Daily, 29 June) projects **BN 36–42, PH 10–16, PN 2–5** under a 45–50% turnout. FULCRUM (ISEAS) titled its analysis "Advantage Barisan Nasional."

**Green wave / PN among Malay voters:** Weakened but not negligible. PN contests only **33 of 56 seats** and is "openly divided" — PAS and Bersatu are not helping each other. But green-wave penetration is real in specific seats (e.g., Bukit Pasir, where PN nearly doubled its vote to ~11,200/~45%). A PAS directive to back BN in non-PN seats risks backfiring by pushing non-Malay voters to PH.

**BN incumbency & Onn Hafiz:** His main asset is the economic record (6.4% 2025 growth, record RM110bn FDI, RTS Link, JS-SEZ). He contests Machap (a referendum on his leadership) and is playing the "underdog" card. Key risks: the high 40-seat bar, shared economic credit with the federal PH-inclusive government, and the absence of the 2022 Najib "Bossku" boost.

The full structured payload with 20 data points and 14 sources has been recorded.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Election expected 11 July 2026; dissolution announced 1 June 2026 by Onn Hafiz Ghazi; all 56 seats contested; determines whether incumbent BN retains power.

sources.title: The 2026 Johor Election: Advantage Barisan Nasional? | FULCRUM (ISEAS)
sources.snippet: BN favored: reliable ~600,000-vote base, UMNO roots, 6.4% 2025 growth, RM110bn FDI, PN implosion. BN must match 40 seats or it's a strategic failure.

sources.title: Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are double trouble for Anwar | East Asia Forum
sources.snippet: BN incumbent; Onn Hafiz technocratic, RM110bn (US$25.7bn) 2025 investments; PN 'openly divided' but a force able to tap conservative Malay sentiment.

sources.title: The Johor Result That Could Trigger a Snap Election | DagangNews
sources.snippet: Dr Oh Ei Sun: BN to return as government or largest bloc; baseline is retaining 2022 seats; strong PH gains could prompt Anwar to call snap GE. 172 candidates, 2.72m voters.

sources.title: PAS call to back Barisan Nasional in Johor state polls could backfire, say analysts
sources.snippet: PAS directive (28 June) to back BN could galvanize non-Malay support for PH; PN contests 33 of 56 seats; 2022: BN 40, PH 12, PN 3.

sources.title: 程盟凯：柔州州选"选情告急战术" 依然有用！
sources.snippet: Forecast under 45-50% turnout: BN 36-42 seats, PH 10-16, PN 2-5; low turnout favors BN grassroots.

sources.title: 戴子豪：今天柔州的情况和2022年大不同
sources.snippet: 2026 differs from 2022: no Najib 'Bossku' effect; turnout expected above 54.92%; UMNO wins most Malay votes, PH most Chinese votes.

sources.title: Johor State Election Nomination Closes With Heavyweights Set For Contests | TRP
sources.snippet: Nomination closed 27 June; BN and PH contest all 56, PN 33, Bersama 15, MUDA-PSM 5; 2,727,926 voters; 2022: BN 40, PH 12, PN 3, MUDA 1.

sources.title: 张立德.柔森州选：国盟（伊党+土团）表现会更佳？
sources.snippet: PN holds 3 Johor seats; 2022 PN 24% vote share vs PH 26.4%; 3-6% swings could flip Bukit Pasir (198 votes), Segamat (699 votes).

sources.title: 伊党拒助选，土团党能否拿下武吉巴西？ | Malaysiakini
sources.snippet: In Bukit Pasir PN vote rose from 5,850 (Mar 2022) to over 11,200 (~45% share); BN base fell by 800+ votes.

sources.title: Tougher contests expected in Johor | The Star
sources.snippet: 172 candidates (down from 239); 14 straight, 27 three-cornered, 12 four-cornered, 3 five-cornered; 29 seats for majority; ~2.7m voters.

sources.title: 柔佛州选自认处于劣势，国阵聚焦密集接触选民 | Malaysiakini
sources.snippet: Onn Hafiz calls BN the 'underdog' since it is not the federal government; intensifying voter outreach before 11 July.

sources.title: Bersatu voters could back PH out of spite for PAS, says analyst | MYKMU
sources.snippet: PN contests 33 seats (16 Bersatu, 11 PAS); parties not assisting each other; analysts debate whether Bersatu voters may back PH.

sources.title: 柔佛州选：柔国阵竞选宣言出炉 | Oriental Daily
sources.snippet: BN manifesto: 63 commitments, 200,000 jobs, RM100m housing subsidies, theme 'Moving Forward Johor, Maintaining Stability.'
